data.body:
Clients should only provide identity once, at lease creation time. When a lease is bound to a knot, Knots should stamp note and handoff capsule metadata from that lease instead of storing unknown identity fields. Clients must not rehydrate this on read; the stored knot data should already be correct.

Reproduction steps:
1. Create a lease with agent identity populated, for example provider, agent name, model, and model version.
2. Bind that lease to a knot and operate on the knot through the lease.
3. Add a note with `kno update <id> --add-note "..."` without passing note identity flags.
4. Add a handoff capsule with `kno update <id> --add-handoff-capsule "..."` without passing handoff identity flags.
5. Inspect the knot with `kno show <id> --json`.

Expected result:
- The stored note and handoff capsule entries are stamped with the lease-owned identity metadata.
- Username, agentname, model, and version reflect the lease metadata rather than unknown placeholders.
- Clients do not need to supply identity again after lease creation and do not need to repair metadata on read.

Actual result:
- Knots stores the new note and handoff capsule entries with `username`, `agentname`, `model`, and `version` set to `"unknown"` even though the lease already has that identity.
- This forces clients into an invalid position where they would otherwise have to choose between re-sending identity outside lease creation or rehydrating metadata on read.
data.title: Stamp note and handoff metadata from the lease
